data.title: U.S. stocks falls as tech sector struggles, rate-cut uncertainties rattle markets
data.description:
Wall Street's major indexes were lower on Wednesday, as U.S. technology stocks weighed on the markets and uncertainty persisted among investors.

The S&P 500 (SP500) was last -0.5% in late morning trade, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was also -0.7%, and the Dow (DJI) was -0.3%.

“Investors trimmed their exposure following a comment from Fed Chair Jerome Powell [saying] ‘equity prices are fairly highly valued.’ He went on to say that the current situation wasn’t a time ‘… of elevated financial stability risks,’ but investors decided that discretion is the better part of valour and took the opportunity to lighten up a touch,” said David Morrison, senior market analyst at Trade Nation.

On the tech front, OpenAI is considering renting AI chips from Nvidia (NVDA) -1.5% to reduce costs associated with the new $100B data center partnership between the two companies.

“This investment was welcomed in many quarters as evidence of the faith that the most valuable company in history has in the future of generative AI. But others have questioned the circular nature of the investment, particularly as the journey to monetizing the ongoing funding in AI is far from clear,” said Morrison.

“On top of this, the assumption is that Nvidia (NVDA), and other leading chip companies, have an unbridgeable moat around them. But as was demonstrated with DeepSeek at the beginning of this year, it’s quite possible that Chinese companies come up with cheaper alternatives to Nvidia’s products, particularly since U.S. bans on selling specific chips to China are driving Chinese innovation in this area,” he added.

Micron Technology (MU) edged lower -4.2%, even after its outlook eclipsed estimates on AI data center demand. Adobe (ADBE) -3.6% and Oracle (ORCL) -3.8% are also pushing the S&P 500 (SP500) and the Nasdaq (COMP:IND) lower.

Over in the bond market, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 3 basis points to 4.14%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 1 basis point to 3.60%.

On the economy, new home sales surged 20.5% MoM in August to 800k. Consensus was for 649k.

Business uncertainty about revenue growth rose to 3.83% in September, up from 3.77% in August, according to an Atlanta Fed survey. Also, CFOs recorded greater optimism in Q3, the Richmond Fed survey showed.

U.S. stocks ended in the red on Tuesday, breaking from their three-day all-time high closes, after Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell largely reiterated his message from last week that policymakers will have a difficult path ahead on interest rates.

data.title: Guardant partners with Quest to widen access to Shield cancer test
data.description:
• Guardant Health (NASDAQ:GH) and Quest Diagnostics (NYSE:DGX) announced a partnership on Wednesday to broaden the availability of the former’s blood-based non-invasive cancer screening test Shield in the U.S.
  • Under the multi-year partnership, healthcare providers served by Quest (NYSE:DGX) will be able to place orders for Shield through their current Quest account and electronic health record (HER), the companies said.
  • Having served nearly 650,000 clinician and hospital accounts last year, Quest (NYSE:DGX) is expected to enhance patients' access to Shield, which is approved in the U.S. as a primary screening tool to detect colorectal cancer in average-risk adults aged 45 years and above.
  • "By rapidly scaling Shield's lifesaving innovation through Quest's nationwide network, we will strengthen our ability to fundamentally change the trajectory of the disease." Guardant’s (NASDAQ:GH) co-CEO AmirAli Talasaz said.

data.title: Vor Biopharma upgraded to Buy at Stifel on telitacicept prospects
data.description:
Vor Biopharma (NASDAQ:VOR) has been upgraded to Buy from Hold at Stifel as confidence in the company’s long-term potential has grown following prospects for its lead asset, telitacicept.

The shares of the company are up 13% so far today.

The research firm’s optimism is centered on telitacicept with demonstrated clinical success in China and the potential to emerge as a treatment in numerous autoimmune indications. 

After a near 40% decline in VOR shares over the last two months, analysts at Stifel now sees an improved risk/reward setup, especially with several key catalysts anticipated by year-end 2025.

With telitacicept already approved for systemic lupus erythematosus, rheumatoid arthritis, and myasthenia gravis in China, as well as pending regulatory submissions for primary Sjögren’s disease and IgA nephropathy, Stifel analysts views Vor as well positioned to leverage this momentum in more competitive global markets.

Stifel raised price target for the company to $55 from 30 cents.

data.title: CFO optimism brightens in Q3 as uncertainty ebbs: Richmond Fed survey
data.description:
Finance chiefs recorded greater optimism in the third quarter of the year as economic uncertainty faded, though tariffs remain a key concern, according to a survey released by the Federal Reserve Bank of Richmond on Wednesday.

Uncertainty fell in importance to seventh in Q3 from second highest in Q2.

“Likely, it is the fall in uncertainty that helped boost optimism in the third quarter,” said Sonya Ravindranath Waddell, vice president and economist with the Richmond Fed. “The return of optimism and GDP expectations to levels more in line with the beginning of 2024 is reassuring. But concern about tariffs is real and impactful for many CFOs in the survey.”

Tariffs and trade policy were the top concern among survey respondents for the third straight quarter, followed by monetary policy and inflation. 

Without the addition of tariffs, CFOs, on average, would expect price growth of about 30% or lower this year and roughly 25% lower in 2026, signaling firms expect to grapple with tariff-driven price increases into next year.

The survey was also issued by Duke University’s Fuqua School of Business and the Federal Reserve Bank of Atlanta.

data.title: Sunrun deploys first U.S. residential vehicle-to-grid distributed power plant
data.description:
Sunrun (NASDAQ:RUN) +2.3% in Wednesday's trading after saying it activated the first U.S. residential vehicle-to-grid distributed power plant in partnership with Exelon (EXC) utility Baltimore Gas & Electric.

The pilot program is dispatching energy from customers' all-electric Ford F-150 Lightning trucks to the grid during periods of peak demand this summer and fall.

The partnership between Sunrun (NASDAQ:RUN) and Baltimore Gas & Electric launched last summer with vehicle-to-home capability, and the program advanced to pioneering vehicle-to-grid energy dispatch, becoming the first U.S. residential bidirectional electric vehicle distributed power plant.

"This groundbreaking program marks a huge milestone for distributed power plants in America and demonstrates that the large batteries inside electric vehicles can do more than power a commute when technology and policies come together," Sunrun (RUN) said. "They can also stabilize the grid, provide backup power to homes, and lower energy costs for everyone."

data.title: Circle Internet Group joins Russell Indexes
data.description:
Circle Internet Group (NYSE:CRCL) joined the Russell indexes as part of the quarterly initial public offering update.

Shares of the company were up 2% at $133.74 in morning trading on Wednesday.

The company, in June, raised $1.1B in an upsized initial public offering, as the stablecoin issuer priced its shares above the marketed range.

data.title: Planet Fitness set to keep winning in 'golden age of fitness' -- Jefferies
data.description:
Planet Fitness (NYSE:PLNT) is seeing increased memberships across all demographics and is benefiting from post-COVID suburbanization trends, leading to an increased total addressable market (TAM) and prospects for more unit growth, Jefferies’ Randall Konik writes in his research note to clients.

“With the golden age of fitness here, Planet Fitness is set to keep winning,” Konik says, reaffirming his Buy rating on the stock with a price target that assumes 76% upside from Tuesday’s close.

“Planet Fitness’ value-driven, simplified offering continues to resonate, positioning it as a standout brand in a structurally expanding industry,” Konik says.

With a strategy focused on member experience and format optimization, the company is seeing tangible improvements in member engagement and rejoin rates, he says, adding that the expansion to Spain is a “breakout success.”

“In just one year, clubs [in Spain] are ramping like those in the U.S., thanks to boots-on-the-ground execution and a service-oriented model that contrasts with competitors,” contributing to the brand’s rising global recognition and long runway to international growth.

Wall Steet analysts  also view Planet Fitness (NYSE:PLNT) as a Strong Buy with a score of 4.52 out of 5. Seeking Alpha authors give the stock a Buy rating, while the Quant rating of 2.90 out of 5 translates into a Hold. 

Konik’s endorsement is fueling a 6% gain in Planet Fitness (NYSE:PLNT), driving the stock above its 200-day moving average.

data.title: Voyager Technologies added to Russell 2000 Index, Russell 3000 Index
data.description:
Voyager Technologies (NYSE:VOYG) was added to the Russell 2000 and Russell 3000 Indexes, documents from FTSE Russell showed. The additions took effect from September 22.

Russell U.S. indexes add initial public offerings (IPOs) as part of the quarterly review process in March, September, and December. The additions are done to enhance overall market representation.

In the past 15 years, more than 1,700 IPOs have been added to the Russell US indexes, with the majority entering the Russell 2000 Index, which tracks the performance of small-cap U.S. equities, as per FTSE Russell.

The Russell 3000 Index measures the performance of the largest 3,000 U.S. companies, representing around 96% of the investable U.S. equity market. The Russell 2000 comprises the 2,000 smallest companies within the broader Russell 3000 Index.

Voyager’s share rallied, ending 12.74% higher on Monday, following the addition. VOYG was trading 1% lower at $31.53 on Wednesday morning.

data.title: Google says AI Plus subscription to expand to more countries
data.description:
• Google (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) said late on Tuesday that its AI Plus subscription plan will be expanding to 40 countries, as it looks to keep boosting adoption of its viral image generation and editing model, informally known as Nano Banana.
  • The plan includes access to higher limits of the aforementioned model, as well as added access to its video generation model, Veo 3 Fast, directly inside the Gemini app. Other benefits of the subscription include access to its Whisk and Flow Gemini AI image and filmmaking tools; Gemini access built into Gmail, Docs, Sheets and more; Higher limits in NotebookLM; and 200 GB of storage across Photos, Drive and Gmail.
  • The subscription can be shared with five other family members, Google added.
  • Added countries include Angola, Bangladesh, Benin, Bolivia, Ukraine, Vietnam and more. Prices vary by country, but the plan costs $19.99 per month in the U.S.
  • Nano Banana has helped boost usage of Google's Gemini chatbot app, which surpassed OpenAI's ChatGPT as the most downloaded free app on the App Store earlier this month.
  • Google shares fell 0.8% in late morning trading.

data.title: Axon Enterprise rated Overweight in new coverage at Piper Sandler
data.description:
Axon Enterprise (NASDAQ:AXON), best known for its Taser devices, is undergoing a transformation that positions it as a leader in a much broader public safety technology market, according to new research from Piper Sandler. Analyst James Fish late Tuesday initiated coverage with an Overweight rating, citing the company’s innovation pipeline, cross-sell opportunities and strong visibility from recurring revenue.

Axon (NASDAQ:AXON) has expanded its offerings well beyond non-lethal weapons, according to the Sept. 23 report. Years of product development and strategic acquisitions have added body cameras, in-car Fleet systems, connected workflows and intelligence software to its portfolio, boosting the company’s total addressable market (TAM) from about $50 billion in 2022 to $129 billion today.

Roughly 70% of Axon’s (AXON) business now comes from government clients, particularly state and local agencies. Fish highlights this exposure as a strength, pointing to steady budgets and rising investment in law enforcement efficiency. He described the sector as experiencing a “physical security renaissance,” similar to the cybersecurity boom more than a decade ago.

Axon’s (AXON) expansion potential remains significant, with an estimated two billion frontline workers worldwide but only about one million currently using its products. U.S. penetration in state and local markets is less than 15%. A key driver of future growth, the report says, will be upselling existing customers to higher-end Officer Safety Plans that bundle devices with AI-powered software. Piper Sandler estimates this cross-sell and upsell opportunity could generate $4.5 billion in annual recurring revenue, with Axon’s (AXON) Draft One AI assistant alone representing a $600 million market today.

Product refresh cycles should also contribute to growth. The recent launches of Taser 10 and Body 4 have been strong, and major upgrades are expected in the coming years (Taser in 2027–2028, body cameras in 2027 and Fleet in 2028–2029). With more than 95% of customers on subscription contracts, Axon’s (AXON) backlog and ARR provide what Fish calls “visibility for miles,” with potential for revenue to sustain growth above 25% annually through 2027.

While acknowledging that Taser sales growth has slowed and Axon trades at a premium valuation, Fish described it as “one of the few hypergrowth assets in a growth-scarcity environment,” supported by low estimates, strong innovation, and upcoming product refreshes. Piper Sandler set a price target of $893 for Axon (AXON), based on 17.5 times projected 2027 sales.
